prompt: A SentinelReady Offline Training & Field Operations system with main features including learner enrollment governance, resource scheduling, location-managed field placements, route/group package publishing, security simulation training, data quality controls, and an operations analytics back office. The backend exposes Authentication and Identity APIs supporting local username/password login only, role-based access control, and session/token issuance without any external verifiers; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Learner Profile APIs support create/query/update, bulk import (CSV/XLSX up to 10,000 rows per batch), and queue-based enrollment with configurable approval workflows of up to 3 review levels with conditional branching (for example, minors require a second approver and a guardian contact on file). Enrollment status transitions are strictly validated (draft → submitted → under_review → approved/rejected; approved → enrolled or waitlisted; enrolled → cancelled or completed; cancelled may transition to refunded when applicable), and every transition must record actor, timestamp, reason code, and previous/new state. Scheduling APIs provide a general-purpose appointment and waitlist engine with resource models for staff, venues, equipment, and seat-based sessions; time slots use 15-minute increments, bookings require a minimum 2-hour lead time, reschedules are allowed up to 24 hours prior, and cancellations inside 24 hours mark a “late_cancel” event. Conflict detection must prevent double-booking across shared resources, and concurrency anti-oversell uses idempotency keys plus record versioning so that capacity cannot be exceeded; a provisional hold expires after 5 minutes if not confirmed, and waitlist backfill offers the next slot within 10 minutes before expiring to the next person. Location APIs manage rescue points, shelters, and temporary placements using stored coordinates and locally computed distance sorting/geofencing; precise coordinates are visible only to authorized roles, while other roles see an obfuscated location (rounded to 2 decimal degrees) plus a display address label. The service persists in MySQL with Laravel providing the API layer, validation, background jobs, and transactional consistency on a single-machine Docker deployment. Core tables include users, roles, permissions, learners, learner_identifiers (unique indexes on normalized email/phone where present), enrollments, approvals, resources, schedules, bookings, waitlist_entries, locations, routes, route_versions, security_exercises, exercise_attempts, audit_events, and report_definitions; all tables use immutable created_at and audited updated_at plus soft-delete where legally appropriate. Data cleaning and deduplication runs entirely locally: normalization rules standardize dates (MM/DD/YYYY), phone formats, currency in USD, coordinate precision, and ratings ranges; entity disambiguation uses deterministic fingerprints (for learners: normalized name + DOB + last4 phone; for POIs: name + rounded coordinates + address tokens), and versioned updates preserve prior values for replay and audits. The Hands-On Security Training module supports configurable exercises (phishing identification, password strength checks, privacy-setting verification) with explicit scoring rules, replayable action trails, and assignment publishing to cohorts. Non-functional requirements include p95 API latency under 300 ms at 100 requests/second on commodity hardware, strict transactional boundaries for booking/enrollment state changes, tamper-evident audit logging, encryption of sensitive fields at rest, masking of PII in standard responses by default, local report generation with export, and operational observability via structured logs and queryable audit trails—without any dependency on internet connectivity, third-party APIs, cloud storage, push notifications, or external map services.
